categories.1: governed mutation routed through the orchestrator
categories.3: generated public-output mutation outside governed records
categories.6: unacceptable bypass requiring removal or refactoring

classifications.justification: Constitution guardian reads governed records and writes only a generated report file (docs/agent-reports/).
Non-authoritative operational event log writer (kernel/events/log.jsonl). Superseded for execution history by kernel/events/projection.js, which derives canonical events from the ledger.
Phase 2 durable IO primitive: atomic same-directory replace, fsync, and durable unlink used exclusively by the execution/recovery layer.
Phase 2 recovery infrastructure that durably records the canonical pre-state manifest before governed mutation.
Phase 2 recovery infrastructure that durably preserves execution artifacts in the institution-controlled execution state store.
Phase 2 governed mutation session that applies journaled writes and enforces durable recovery barriers.
Phase 2 recovery infrastructure that durably records rollback verification results.
Phase 4 recovery infrastructure that durably records an unproven runtime-isolation restoration and blocks later runtime or direct orchestrator execution.
Phase 2 content-addressed snapshot storage used only by recovery and rollback.
Phase 3 execution infrastructure that durably records validation artifacts bound to committed ledger transitions.
Execution infrastructure wrapper for governed writes; callers remain inside the authoritative execution path.
Phase 2 exclusive execution lock (O_EXCL create/unlink). The sanctioned mutual-exclusion mechanism.
Execution infrastructure rename helper used inside the governed mutation path.
Phase 2 rollback restores pre-state through durable IO within the governed recovery layer.
Phase 2 startup reconciliation of interrupted attempts through the recovery layer.
Writes a generated executive status output; reads governed records but does not mutate them.
Hash-chained append-only log primitive backing the execution ledger, mutation journal, and transaction log.
Creates and cleans disposable writable agent workspaces outside the live institution root and copies only declared regular-file inputs.
Defense-in-depth recovery guard that snapshots, detects, restores, and verifies security-critical governed-tree state after isolated agent execution.

The sole approved production agent-execution adapter. It probes Linux namespace/chroot/seccomp capability, builds the reviewed sandbox launcher, exposes only a disposable workspace, and does not mount the live institution root.

Static sandbox launcher used only by the approved isolation adapter. It drops capabilities, sets no-new-privileges, installs a seccomp filter blocking mount/namespace/root-escape syscalls, and executes the agent from /workspace.
Authoritative runtime integration layer: converts isolated agent output to transaction intent and invokes executeApprovedTransaction; it has no direct governed mutation path.
Legacy diagnostic/dry-run runtime. Active agent execution is permanently disabled and no child-process spawn path or override flag remains.
Writes a generated memory export output; does not mutate governed records.
Writes a generated memory status report; does not mutate governed records.
Writes a generated proposals status output; does not mutate governed records.
Writes generated ADS export/report outputs under public/ and docs/agent-reports/; no governed-record mutation.
Operational tooling that launches a local archive assistant process; no governed-record mutation.
Writes a generated archive manifest output.
Generates archive manifest output files; not a governed record.
Publication generator producing public/ assets; generated output outside governed records.
Publication build finalize step writing generated public/ output.
Publication build IO helper writing generated public/ output.
Writes generated QA status into public data outputs; not a governed record.
This audit tool writes a generated report to docs/bypass-audit-report.json (outside governed records).
Legacy agent writing only a generated report; no governed-record mutation.
Exports ADS records to public/data generated output.
Generates a derived section-content data file; generated output.
Generates public publication assets under public/; generated output outside governed records.
Institutional QA: reads records, spawns validate-ads, and writes generated QA status into public data outputs.
Assembles a release candidate under release/ and public/data generated outputs.
Reads governed records for release checks and writes only a generated report; no governed-record mutation.
Legacy analysis agent that writes only a generated report (docs/agent-reports/). Migrating to the transactional runtime is future work; it does not mutate governed records.
Legacy agent writing only a generated report; no governed-record mutation.
